items.title: Exploring the genetic heritage of Peru: an interview with Victor Borda
items.content: Dr. Victor Borda is a Research Associate at the University of Maryland, School of Medicine, and the Institute for Health Computing. In this Q&A, we discuss his recent publication in Communications Biology on admixture and genetic diversity across Peru, his work with the Peruvian Genome Project, and the relevance of developing genomic resources in Latin America.

items.title: Reduced inhibition, bursting, and accelerated oscillations drive early hippocampal hyperactivity in Alzheimer’s disease in vivo
items.author: Jian Gan
items.content: Early hippocampal hyperactivity in AD before Aβ plaque deposition could be explained in vivo by reduced inhibitory synaptic transmission with enhanced pyramidal cell bursting and accelerated neural oscillations driven by increased theta/beta rhythms.

items.title: Accurate protein-protein interactions modeling through physics-informed geometric invariant learning
items.author: Shuangjia Zheng
items.content: ProTact, an SE(3)-invariant geometric GNN, enhances protein-protein contact prediction and docking by integrating physics-informed constraints. It surpasses existing methods in low MSA scenarios and enhances AlphaFold3’s confidence scores.

items.title: Reversal of ATP synthase is a key attribute accompanying cellular differentiation of Trypanosoma brucei insect forms
items.author: Alena Zíková
items.content: The ATP synthase–IF1 regulatory axis is involved in metabolic remodeling and life-cycle progression in Trypanosoma brucei.

items.title: HarveST uses a heterogeneous graph learning framework to reveal spatial transcriptomics patterns
items.author: Yanlin Zhang
items.content: HarveST is a computational method that uses a weighted heterogeneous graph learning framework to integrate spatial and transcriptomic data, improving spatial domain identification and marker gene detection in complex tissues.

items.title: Dynamic gut responses to sepsis uncovered by multi-omics profiling in a rodent model
items.author: Chuanzheng Sun
items.content: Dynamic multi-omics profiling reveals coordinated immune, epithelial and microbiome reprogramming in the gut during sepsis progression.

items.title: Chemical programming of kinase inhibitors in a modular chemputer-based system
items.author: Ross Cagan
items.content: Chemical programming on a modular Chemputer digital chemistry platform couples automated synthesis with Drosophila phenotypic screening, yielding AP4-43, a multi-kinase inhibitor lead that improved survival in a KRAS-mutant colorectal cancer model.

items.title: ALDOB K87 lactylation drives mitochondrial fission and metabolic reprogramming in pulmonary hypertension
items.author: Lina Shan
items.content: Hypoxia-induced ALDOB lactylation amplifies glycolysis-derived lactate production, creating a self-reinforcing loop that sustained lactylation and mitochondrial fragmentation in pulmonary hypertension.
